# GeneradorQR

A simple desktop application for Windows that generates a QR code from a URL or any text and saves it as a .png image.

## Download the portable version

If you only want to use the program, download `GeneradorQR.exe` from GitHub Releases.

## How to use the application

1. Open `GeneradorQR.exe`.

2. Enter a URL or any text.

3. Click `Generar QR`.

4. The QR code will appear in the window.

5. The .png image will be saved in the same folder as the program.

## Generated file name

The application uses the entered text as the basis for the file name.

For example, if you type:

```text
https://example.com/test
```

The file will be saved with a name similar to:

```text
https___example.com_test.png
```

This happens because Windows doesn't allow certain characters in filenames, so the program automatically replaces them to avoid saving errors.

... ## Requirements for Working with Source Code

If you want to run or compile the project from source code, you need:

- Windows 10 or 11
- Python 3.11 or higher
- An internet connection to install dependencies

## Preparing the Development Environment

If you're going to work on the project from scratch on a new PC, follow these steps:

### 1. Install Python 3.11

Download it from the official website:

`https://www.python.org/downloads/windows/`

### 2. Download the project from GitHub

You can download the repository as a ZIP file and extract it to any folder.

## Running the app from source code

To open the application from source code, double-click on:

```text
run.bat
```


## Project structure

- `app.py`: main application
- `run.bat`: opens the app from source code without displaying the console
- `build.bat`: generates the portable `.exe` version
- `requirements.txt`: dependencies needed to run and compile the project

Automatically generated files:

- `build/`: temporary files from the compilation process
- `dist/`: folder where the final executable is located
- `*.spec`: file generated by PyInstaller
- `*.png`: QR codes created by the application

These files do not need to be uploaded to GitHub.

## Compile the portable version

If you want to generate the portable version to share, double-click on:

```text
build.bat
```

When finished, the executable will be located at:

```text
dist\GeneradorQR.exe
```

This is the file you can upload to GitHub Releases so others can download it directly.
